title: viola tricolor var. derelicta
intro: Viola tricolor var. derelicta, also known as Heartsease or Wild Pansy, is a perennial plant that belongs to the violet family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Viola tricolor var. derelicta include soothing digestive issues, reducing anxiety and stress, and providing relief from skin irritations and wounds. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by infusing the flowers, leaves, and roots in hot water or oil.
